attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who capitalizes on extreme gradients (30%+ pitches) and slight uphill finishes. Attacks inside final kilometer when gradient favors persistence over pure power.
team_tactics: Primarily functions as domestique doing preparatory positioning work for teammates in breaks. When given freedom, commits fully to all-in pacing strategies rather than measured efforts.
terrain_preference: Excels on extreme gradient climbs (30%+ walls like Los Machucos) and slight uphill sprint finishes. Rolling stages suit his positioning work; sustained steep pitches expose pure climbers.
vulnerability: Limited observations show success against cracking favorites rather than head-to-head superiority. Likely struggles when forced to respond to accelerations rather than setting tempo.
key_patterns: Wins from breakaways on summit finishes with extreme gradient changes (2 instances)
Effective all-in pacing strategy on climbs rather than reactive racing (2 instances)
Capable late-race accelerations inside final kilometer on uphill finishes (1 documented win)
Transitions between domestique duties and personal opportunities within same stage race (2017-2018 Vuelta)
form_trajectory: neutral: Showed winning form in 2017 Vuelta mountain stage, returned to domestique duties by 2018 Vuelta with one opportunistic Poland win between
